question_state: The question asks for the highest daily gold spot price (USD/troy oz) during May 13-15, 2026, using Kitco as the resolution source. Gold is trading near $4,702 on May 13 with an intraday high of $4,727.60. The 50-day MA at $4,749 is the key resistance level that gold has failed to break for 4 consecutive sessions. Hot CPI data on May 12 created a bearish fundamental backdrop. The forecast of $4,755 reflects a modest bounce testing but barely exceeding the 50-day MA, with medium confidence given the uncertainty around whether gold will reach that resistance at all.

evidence_surfaced.source: Kitco 24-hour spot chart + Yahoo Finance GC=F data
evidence_surfaced.claim: May 13 Kitco spot high at $4,727.60; GC=F futures high at $4,734.80 — current day's high already established

evidence_surfaced.source: FXEmpire CMT technical analysis + calculate_technical_indicators tool
evidence_surfaced.claim: 50-day MA at $4,749 and 100-day MA at $4,789 define key resistance; gold has failed 4 consecutive sessions to break above $4,749-$4,773 zone
Hot CPI data (May 12) crushed Fed cut bets, triggering gold sell-off from $4,765 to $4,677; bearish fundamental backdrop

evidence_surfaced.source: RoboForex weekly forecast + FXEmpire range analysis
evidence_surfaced.claim: Baseline scenario is sideways consolidation in $4,500-$4,900 range for the week of May 11-15
RSI at 49.8 — neutral territory, no overbought/oversold signal

judgment_trajectory: Initial scan: gold near $4,702 with 50-day MA at $4,749 as key overhead resistance
After CPI context: hot CPI on May 12 triggered sell-off; bearish fundamental pressure suggests upside capped
After analyst forecasts: RoboForex and FXEmpire both project sideways consolidation, supporting a modest bounce scenario
Final synthesis: weighted scenario analysis (35% bearish, 45% sideways, 20% bullish) yields ~$4,754, rounded to $4,755 — just above 50-day MA resistance
what_worked: Using calculate_technical_indicators to get precise MA levels (50-day at $4,749, 20-day at $4,648) anchored the resistance/support analysis
Finding RoboForex weekly forecast covering the exact May 11-15 window provided a directly relevant analyst baseline
Kitco 24-hour spot chart gave the current-day high as a concrete floor for the forecast
Weighted scenario analysis in Python forced disciplined probability assignment rather than anchoring on a single narrative
what_misled: Long-term forecasts from Morgan Stanley and SSGA ($5,000+ targets) were not relevant to a 3-day window and could have inflated expectations if over-weighted
Artifact path denial forced re-fetching stock data, wasting a round of tool calls
unresolved_concerns: May 13 session not yet closed — intraday high could change before settlement
Geopolitical wildcards (Iran deal progress) could shift safe-haven demand unpredictably
Whether gold will actually test the $4,749 resistance on May 14-15 or continue drifting lower on persistent USD strength
Kitco spot vs. futures basis (~$2-5) could affect the exact reported daily high
